title: Living in Boulder Vlog 2 - 29 - 2016  I have Skin Cancer

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares that he was diagnosed with well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma (skin cancer) after getting his biopsy results and stitches removed at a Denver dermatology clinic. He traces the likely cause to a severe sunburn in Indonesia in 1998 and explains he'll need a removal procedure on the 18th plus biannual full-body checkups going forward. He also briefly mentions fixing something at home and needing to take his failing computer in for repair.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Relieved it's the slow-spreading kind; not worried about his prognosis
Will apply SPF 30+ sunscreen every three hours and stay covered for the rest of his life
Regrets waiting five months to get the sore checked out; acknowledges he was lazy

transcript.text: Hey, what's up? This is Living in Boulder Vlog with Kyle Pounds, February 29th Leap Day.
There's only one February 29th every four years.
So, on this day, I had to go into my, I'm going to minimize this.
I went into the Denver Dermatology, and I got my results, or I had my stitches taken out, and then I got my results from biopsy.
So, I have well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma, so I have skin cancer, but it's the kind that doesn't metastasize very quickly.
So, but I have a, I can feel it. He took a big chunk out, and I can still feel I have a chunk in there.
So, on the 18th, they're going to take the, they're going to take the tumor out.
So, you get these because if you, it's usually in men's, I guess, and it can start in their 40s, but I got it young.
Usually, it starts in their 50s, but, or, it can start in their 50s usually, but most of the guys who get it are in their 70s.
So, so now on, for the rest of my life, I have to put on 30 SPF sunscreen and keep myself covered all the time.
And when I go out, I have to apply the sunscreen every three hours.
And if I sweat, I have to reapply it.
So, just get waterproof sunscreen and keep it on all the time.
I was, I was fearing this.

See, it happens if you get too much sun exposure, or I think if you have too many accidents in the sun.
And yeah, I had a really bad accident in the sun back in like 98.

And I canoed like this 300-pound canoe or huge canoe around Lake Toba, halfway around it with this, or like 40% around it with this kid, this English kid.
